channel.title: Operating Systems on Scorpiosec
channel.description: Recent content in Operating Systems on Scorpiosec

item.title: Demystifying the Linux File System Hierarchy
item.description: Linux File Hierarchy Structure Proficiency in Linux is indispensable for cybersecurity professionals, you may have noticed that Linux is different from Windows in the way files are organized. The Filesystem Hierarchy Standard (FHS) maintained by the Linux Foundation provides a model for the directories structure. While there may be some variations across Linux distributions, they typically adhere to this standard. To enhance your Linux skills, a fundamental understanding of the file system is essential.
